    Indicates how and when an option can be exercised, either American (exercise any time before expiration) or European (exercise only at expiration).

    The date of the option contract this is the value in the OSI and is typically the same as the expiration_date but can vary when the option expiration is accelerated.

    The date of the option contract this is the value in the OSI and is typically the same as the expiration_date but can vary when the option expiration is accelerated.
    Option.Builder
    settlementMethod(SettlementMethod settlementMethod)
    Specifies how an option is settled at expiration, either Physical (delivery of the underlying asset) or Cash (payment of the cash difference).
    Option.Builder
    settlementMethod(Optional<? extends SettlementMethod> settlementMethod)
    Specifies how an option is settled at expiration, either Physical (delivery of the underlying asset) or Cash (payment of the cash difference).
    Option.Builder
    settlementType(SettlementType settlementType)
    Indicates whether an option follows Standard settlement terms or has Non-Standard terms, often due to adjustments like corporate actions.
    Option.Builder
    settlementType(Optional<? extends SettlementType> settlementType)
    Indicates whether an option follows Standard settlement terms or has Non-Standard terms, often due to adjustments like corporate actions.

  • Method Details

    • callPut

      public Option.Builder callPut(CallPut callPut)
      Indicates whether the option is a Call or a Put. A Put entitles the holder to sell shares at the specified strike_price, while a Call entitles the holder to buy shares at the specified strike_price.

    • callPut

      public Option.Builder callPut(Optional<? extends CallPut> callPut)
      Indicates whether the option is a Call or a Put. A Put entitles the holder to sell shares at the specified strike_price, while a Call entitles the holder to buy shares at the specified strike_price.

    • exerciseStyle

      public Option.Builder exerciseStyle(ExerciseStyle exerciseStyle)
      Indicates how and when an option can be exercised, either American (exercise any time before expiration) or European (exercise only at expiration).

    • exerciseStyle

      public Option.Builder exerciseStyle(Optional<? extends ExerciseStyle> exerciseStyle)
      Indicates how and when an option can be exercised, either American (exercise any time before expiration) or European (exercise only at expiration).

    • expirationDate

      public Option.Builder expirationDate(AssetExpirationDate expirationDate)
      The date when the option contract expires. This will typically match the series_date but can vary when the option expiration is accelerated for a given series. This field will represent the actual expiration date.

    • expirationDate

      public Option.Builder expirationDate(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AssetExpirationDate> expirationDate)
      The date when the option contract expires. This will typically match the series_date but can vary when the option expiration is accelerated for a given series. This field will represent the actual expiration date.

    • optionRoot

      public Option.Builder optionRoot(OptionRoot optionRoot)
      The full option root information

    • optionRoot

      public Option.Builder optionRoot(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionRoot> optionRoot)
      The full option root information

    • optionType

      public Option.Builder optionType(OptionType optionType)
      Type of deliverable asset the option is set to deliver upon expiry.

    • optionType

      public Option.Builder optionType(Optional<? extends OptionType> optionType)
      Type of deliverable asset the option is set to deliver upon expiry.

    • rootSymbol

      public Option.Builder rootSymbol(String rootSymbol)
      The leading segment of the OSI, which is typically the original deliverable symbol of the option. There are frequent exceptions to this rule, such as when there are multiple deliverables or the deliverable goes through a corporate action.

    • rootSymbol

      public Option.Builder rootSymbol(Optional<String> rootSymbol)
      The leading segment of the OSI, which is typically the original deliverable symbol of the option. There are frequent exceptions to this rule, such as when there are multiple deliverables or the deliverable goes through a corporate action.

    • seriesDate

      public Option.Builder seriesDate(SeriesDate seriesDate)
      The date of the option contract this is the value in the OSI and is typically the same as the expiration_date but can vary when the option expiration is accelerated.

    • seriesDate

      public Option.Builder seriesDate(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends SeriesDate> seriesDate)
      The date of the option contract this is the value in the OSI and is typically the same as the expiration_date but can vary when the option expiration is accelerated.

    • settlementMethod

      public Option.Builder settlementMethod(SettlementMethod settlementMethod)
      Specifies how an option is settled at expiration, either Physical (delivery of the underlying asset) or Cash (payment of the cash difference).

    • settlementMethod

      public Option.Builder settlementMethod(Optional<? extends SettlementMethod> settlementMethod)
      Specifies how an option is settled at expiration, either Physical (delivery of the underlying asset) or Cash (payment of the cash difference).

    • settlementType

      public Option.Builder settlementType(SettlementType settlementType)
      Indicates whether an option follows Standard settlement terms or has Non-Standard terms, often due to adjustments like corporate actions.

    • settlementType

      public Option.Builder settlementType(Optional<? extends SettlementType> settlementType)
      Indicates whether an option follows Standard settlement terms or has Non-Standard terms, often due to adjustments like corporate actions.

    • strikePrice

      public Option.Builder strikePrice(StrikePrice strikePrice)
      The price at which the option holder can buy or sell the deliverable asset(s)

    • strikePrice

      public Option.Builder strikePrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends StrikePrice> strikePrice)
      The price at which the option holder can buy or sell the deliverable asset(s)
